taxonomy: Nephrodium canariense is a species of extinct fern that belongs to the genus Nephrodium, which is a part of the family Matoniaceae. This plant is classified within the division Pteridophyta and the kingdom Plantae. It was a type of vascular plant that was widely distributed in the past and is now considered to be extinct. The classification of Nephrodium canariense is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its frond structure and spore-bearing organs. The exact classification of this plant is still a subject of study and research in the field of paleobotany.
uses: Nephrodium canariense, also known as the Canary Island polypody, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. In addition, Nephrodium canariense has been used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and even snake bites. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.
